---
title: "{{< meta course_title >}}"
deck_id: d04
deck_title: "{{< meta decks.d04.title >}}"
deck_short_title: "{{< meta decks.d04.short_title >}}"

include-after-body:
  - text: |
      <script id="deck-nav-meta" type="application/json">
      {
        "deck_prev_file": "{{< meta decks.d03.file >}}",
        "deck_prev_title": "{{< meta decks.d03.title >}}",
        "deck_next_file": "{{< meta decks.d05.file >}}",
        "deck_next_title": "{{< meta decks.d05.title >}}"
      }
      </script>

subtitle: |
  <span class="deck-title">{{< meta decks.d04.title >}}</span><br>
  <span class="deck-meta">{{< meta texts.cb.short >}} Ch. 11<br> 
  Test 2 on 4/7 on <br>
  <span class="indent"> Optimality of Estimators, </span> <br> 
  <span class="indent">Hypothesis Testing, & </span> <br>
  <span class="indent">Ordinary Least Squares Through Inference</span>
  </span>
  </span>
---

# Linear Models

<h3>Until now</h3>
- We have been focused on inference for population parameters, such as $\mu$, $\sigma^2$, $p$, $\lambda$, ...

- Nothing influenced our random variable $Y$ except for the randomness in the data generating process

<h3>Moving forward</h3>

- [Response/Output/Dependent Variable]{.alert} $Y$

- [Explained/Predicted]{.alert} by a vector of [Predictors/Explanatory Variables/Inputs/Independent Variables]{.alert} $\vx$

  - The $x_j$ may be 
  
    - Functions of other variables or each other

    - Categorical variables encoded as dummy variables

- $\Ex(Y \mid \vx)$ depends on $\vx$ through a linear function $\vx^\top \vbeta$, where $\vbeta$ is a vector of unknown parameters 


## Types of linear models

- [Ordinary least squares (OLS)](#lsqregress) $Y = \vx^\top \vbeta + \varepsilon$ with $\Ex(\varepsilon) = 0$ and $\cov(\varepsilon) = \sigma^2 \mI$

  - [Solution, geometry, unbiasedness](#lsqregress)

  - [Inference for model parameters and predictions](#ols_inference)

  - [Diagnostics](#ols_diagnostics)

  - [Model selection](#ols_selection)


- [One-way analysis of variance (ANOVA)](#oneway_anova) $Y_{ij} = \mu_i + \varepsilon_{ij}$ with $\Ex(\varepsilon_{ij}) = 0$ and $\cov(\varepsilon_{ij}) = \sigma^2 \mI$

- [Logistic regression](#logistic-glm) and other generalized linear models

- [Gaussian process regression](#gauss_process) [non-parametric method]{.alert} where $Y = f(\vx) + \varepsilon$ with $\Ex(\varepsilon) = 0$ and $\cov(\varepsilon) = \sigma^2 \mI$ and $f$ is a Gaussian process




{{< include ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/shared/statistics/linear/linear_frame.qmd >}}

{{< include ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/shared/statistics/linear/lsqregressfit.qmd >}}

{{< include ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/shared/statistics/linear/lsqregressinfer.qmd >}}

{{< include ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/shared/statistics/linear/lsqregressselect.qmd >}}

{{< include ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/shared/statistics/linear/oneway_anova.qmd >}}

{{< include ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/shared/statistics/linear/logistic_glm.qmd >}}

{{< include ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/shared/statistics/linear/gauss_process.qmd >}}
